# SRC012 - The Online Display Ad Effectiveness Funnel & Carryover

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Measurement |
| Authors / source | Johnson, Lewis & Nubbemeyer |
| Year | 2017 |
| Source type | Meta-study of 432 field experiments |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/Johnson-Garrett-PAPER-VERSION-2.pdf](https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/Johnson-Garrett-PAPER-VERSION-2.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Google Display context; display-specific.

## Core takeaway

Display can affect site visits and conversions; power and carryover matter.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to define minimum sample sizes and carryover windows before calling a result.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC012
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
